title: citharexylum kunthianum
intro: Citharexylum kunthianum, also known as the "Boracay buttonwood" or "Boracay button bush," is a type of flowering plant in the Verbena family, native to the Philippines and Indonesia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Citharexylum kunthianum include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
